series_description: Bond files of state issuances administered by the Commissioners of the Sinking Fund. Such files may include, but are not limited to: transcripts of the bond proceedings, official statements, bond orders, trust agreements, pricing books, and other supporting documentation. Records in this series are stored together in one file that corresponds to each separate issuance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper version until the earlier of (a) 20 years after final maturity of the original bond or corresponding refunding or restructuring of that bond, or (b) an electronic copy of the transcript is created. ; Electronic: Retain for 20 years after final maturity of the original bond or corresponding refunding or restructuring of that bond.

series_description: Various reports requested by on line banking procedures.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_description: Contains results of criminal records checks conducted by the Ohio bureau of criminal identification and investigation, which may include records from the federal bureau of investigation. Also includes correspondence from the Ohio bureau of criminal investigation and investigation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year after employee''s date of separation

series_description: May include, but not necessarily limited to: service records, operations maintenace reports, operating manual, repair records, and usage logs.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain during period of vehicle ownership ; Electronic: Retain during period of vehicle ownership

series_description: Charts outlining the reporting structure of employees within the office of the Treasurer of State.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded. ; Paper: Retain until superseded.

series_title: Employee FMLA, Disability, Worker Comp records
series_description: Records pertaining to Family Medical Leave Act (FMLA), Disability & Workers Compensation claims filed by employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 years after employee separation ; Electronic: Retain 6 years after employee separation

series_description: Records relating to requests filed by the Treasurer of State with the Controlling Board.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image for 4 years

series_title: Daily Packet for Investments in State Regular Account
series_description: Documents that may be saved electronically include, but are not limited to, the following:(1) Interest and Maturity Income Due, which shows which investments mature (either partially or completely) on the particular day; (2) Daily Transaction Summary, which contains all the transactions executed on a particular date, lists the fund number, discount rate, type of investment, par amount, and principal amount; and (3) Daily Settlement Report, which lists all investments that settle on any particular date.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Daily Packet of Trade Sheets & Trade Report
series_description: This packet contains copies of the trade tickets that document the purchase and sale of securities by employees in the Investments department (including for the State Regular Account & STAR Ohio). The packet also contains a summary Trade Report that lists all securities that were bought, sold, or available for loan to others on the corresponding date.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Files may include, but are not necessarily limited to: copy of civil service application, personnel actions, correspondence, benefit information, resignation documents, performance evaluations, new hire information, disciplinary actions (unless previously expunged), training certificates & position descriptions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain entire file onsite for 6 years after employee’s date of separation. After 6 years, review file and shred contents except for (a) civil service application, (b) personnel action forms, (c) PERS registration, (d) child support compliance forms, (e) acknowledgment of unclassified status (if applicable), and (f) documents detailing circumstances of separation (if any). These items can be shredded upon creation of a digital copy that is checked for accuracy and completeness. ; Electronic: Retain digitized portion of the paper-based file for 50 years from employee''s date of separation

series_description: This includes, but is not limited to, written reprimands, grievance forms, summaries of any hearings held to discuss disciplinary actions or grievances, documents discussing how a grievance was resolved, and notices of appeals to the Personnel Board of Review.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is closed ; Electronic: Retain 5 years after case is closed

series_description: Draft versions of written materials produced by all departments within the Treasurer of State. Drafts may include: RFPs, RFQs, RFIs, written agreements, spreadsheets, memorandum and correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until next draft version is created. Once final version is distributed, destroy all draft versions. ; Paper: Retain until next draft version is created. Once final version is distributed, destroy all draft versions.

series_description: Written agreements that are signed by or on behalf of the office of the Treasurer of State. This also includes any amendments or required notices pertaining to such agreements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract. ; Paper: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract.

series_description: Copies of requests for public records received by the office of the Treasurer of State, and correspondence with requestors in response to the original request.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for two years after the completion of the Public Records request ; Paper: Retain for two years after the completion of the Public Records request

series_description: Bi-weekly scanned paper record submitted by each employee that documents hours worked, leave time used, and overtime/compensatory hours earned.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain images until after annual audit for corresponding fiscal year is performed by Auditor of State, the audit report is released, and all discrepancies are resolved

series_title: Responses to RFPs and related documents
series_description: Copies of bids submitted by companies in response to Requests for Proposals (RFPs), Request for Qualifications (RFQs), and Requests for Information (RFIs) published by the office of the Treasurer of State. Also includes documents used to evaluate the various bid submissions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract for which the response to the RFP / RFI / RFQ was submitted. ; Electronic: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract for which the response to the RFP / RFI / RFQ was submitted.

series_description: Bi-weekly payroll journals list for each employee in the agency: (a) the number of hours worked, (b) gross and net amount of paycheck, and (c) description of each paycheck deduction (grouped by employee and deduction type).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image 5 years ; Microform: Retain microfilm onsite for 50 years from the payroll period referenced in the report

series_description: Requests for Proposals (RFPs), Request for Qualifications (RFQs), and Requests for Information (RFIs) that are published by the office of the Treasurer of State. These documents describe the products and services that the office of the Treasurer of State wishes to purchase, and provide instructions on how companies can submit bids for contracts to provide such products and services.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract for which the RFP / RFI / RFQ is soliciting bids.

series_description: Nightly backup of all entries made into Treasurer of State computer software that runs on the AS400 server. This includes entries made by the Accounting and Revenue Management departments, in the Adtek BankMate software.
retention_statement: Electronic: 8 weeks

series_description: Monthly backup of all entries made into Treasurer of State computer software that runs on the AS400 server. This includes entries made by the Accounting and Revenue Management departments, in the Adtek BankMate software.
retention_statement: Electronic: 2 years

series_description: An .xml file that contains data on warrants presented to the Treasurer of State for payment, and which were originally issued on behalf of the Ohio Department of Jobs and Family Services (ODJFS). This file is sent daily from the Treasurer of State to ODJFS through File Transfer Protocol (FTP). The individual warrants that comprise this file are retained separately pursuant to agency series number ACC-73; series authorization number 090-0309. Retention schedule reflects preference of ODJFS as communicated to Treasurer of State.
retention_statement: Electronic: 1 day

series_title: Daily .xml warrant file for agencies other than ODJFS.
series_description: .xml files that contain data on warrants presented to the Treasurer of State for payment, and which were originally issued on behalf of agencies other than the Ohio Department of Jobs and Family Services (ODJFS). This file is sent daily from the Treasurer of State to the respective agency through File Transfer Protocol (FTP). The individual warrants that comprise this file are retained separately pursuant to agency series number ACC-73; series authorization number 090-0309.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years from date file is sent by FTP

series_description: These files serve as an additional back-up of daily entries made by the Accounting and Revenue Management departments, in the Adtek BankMate software.
retention_statement: Electronic: 1 week

series_description: Files and automated logs that record employee data entry, internet usage, and system login / logoff data.
retention_statement: Electronic: 30 days

series_description: Requests for assistance with software and hardware provided by the Information Technology department of the Treasurer of State.
retention_statement: Electronic: 2 years after tracking ticket is closed

series_description: Daily audit-trail backup files of all entries made into Treasurer of State computer software that runs on the AS400 server. This includes entries made by the Accounting and Revenue Management departments in the Adtek BankMate software. Such entries show the user name and time that a file was opened, created, deleted, and saved.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain one day on AS400

series_description: Warrant processing files received each day from the bank that has the contract to handle warrant processing services for the Treasurer of State. This file consists of images and associated data for warrants presented for payment on the previous day. The images and data are extracted from this data and copies are loaded into the warrant processing software (see agency series number ACC-73; series authorization number 090-0309).
retention_statement: Electronic: 45 days

series_description: Emails and other documents discussing or pertaining to pending audit engagements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until final report for corresponding audit is released

series_description: The schedules, analyses, research, memoranda, narratives, and other documents prepared by the Internal Audit department during assurance and consulting engagements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from date of issuance of corresponding audit report

series_description: Reports that summarize assurance and consulting work performed by the Internal Audit department, and contain recommendations or conclusions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from date of issuance

series_description: All emails that are sent to or from the internet domain "tos.ohio.gov" are retained via a legal hold rule in Office 365.
retention_statement: Electronic: Withinn Office 365 TOS emails have a 1 year legal hold on them to prevent acidental deletion. Searches on deleted emails need to be done from that module in O365.

series_title: Responses to RFPs / RFQs / RFIs for debt issuance and management services
series_description: Copies of bids submitted by companies in response to Requests for Proposals (RFPs), Request for Qualifications (RFQs), and Requests for Information (RFIs) published by or on behalf of the Commissioners of the Sinking Fund, Department of Debt Management, or other offices whose operations were merged into either of these entities.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract or closing of transaction for which the response to the RFP / RFI / RFQ was submitted. For RFQ / RFI responses that do not not pertain to a fixed term contract, retain for two years after new RFQ / RFI for same or similar services is published. If no subsequent RFQ / RFI is published for same or similar services

series_title: Phone calls and voice messages placed to the Ohio Electronic Funds Transfer System
series_description: Recordings of conversations between taxpayers and operators or the voicemail account at the Ohio Electronic Funds Transfer System telephone number. Also includes log of recorded calls. Recordings are retrievable if requestor provides date and approximate time of call, phone number that call was placed from, whether caller spoke with an operator or left a message, and if applicable, the name of the operator with whom the caller spoke.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for one year from date of recording

series_description: Advices are letters sent by a bank to its customer that document and explain adjustments that a bank makes to a customer''s account. The Accounting department maintains advices received on behalf of the State Regular Account. Such advices include, but are not limited to: bank charge slips, returns, confirmations of transactions, records of receipts and disbursements, and monthly account statements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for current fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain for current fiscal year.

series_description: This is a copy of the daily ledger report of state funds. Pursuant to R.C. 113.13, this document must be made available to the Governor and director of the Office of Budget & Management. This report contains information concerning the amount of funds (if any) in the inactive account and active account, as well as the amount of cash on hand. This report also lists the total daily investments balance, which is comprised of interim deposits (both structured and regular) and special purpose investments. Copies of the daily ledger are available online at http://www.tos.ohio.gov/dailyledger/. Paper versions of the ledger are also stored in the State Funds Daily Packet.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_description: This packet contains documents that the Accounting department uses to support transactions posted to the general ledger for the State Regular Account on a given business day. Contents of this folder include, but are not necessarily limited to: 1. pay-in documents accompanying deposits made by state agencies; 2. cash transfer letters that instruct the Revenue Management department to transfer funds from various accounts into the State Regular Account; 3. warrant settlement reports that describe total warrants paid against the State Regular Account on a given day; 4. daily ledger report of state funds; 5. reports listing daily investment activities performed on behalf of State accounts and the STAR Ohio fund; 6. Balance sheets that document the cash activity and balances for each TOS cashier drawer; 7. returned check logs and reports; 8. reports showing ZBA transfers to and from the State Regular Account; 9. wire transfer forms requesting the transfer of funds to non-state accounts; and 10. autopost reports that show how transactions processed by the Treasury Services, Investments, and Trust departments are automatically interfaced and posted to the general ledger. The autopost also includes checks generated by the state checkwriter and ACH transactions. This packet also includes any documents prepared while reconciling general ledger entries for a particular date.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_description: This packet contains documents that the Accounting department uses to support transactions posted to the general ledgers for Custodial Accounts on a given business day. Examples of custodial accounts include accounts owned by the state pension funds, Bureau of Workers Compensation, as well as the Treasurers Custodial Contingency Account (TCCA) (see R.C. 113.10). Contents of this folder include, but are not necessarily limited to: 1. custodial deposit forms accompanying deposits made by custodial account holders; 2. custodial withdrawal forms and letters requesting that that the Treasury Services department transfer funds from custodial accounts via wire, ACH, automatic payment, or checkwriter; 3. custodial transfer forms and letters requesting the transfer of funds from one custodial account to another custodial account or the State Regular Account; 4. adjustment request forms submitted when changes are made to general ledger entries due to errors in dollar amounts or account numbers in previously submitted forms; 5. bank adjustment forms that show changes made to the general ledger due to returned items or bank errors; 6. check cancellation / stop payment forms submitted by custodial agencies; 7. Trust reports that show transactions posted to custodial accounts due to investment-related activities; and 8. Checkwriter reports that contain information on checks issued on a given day on behalf of a custodial account. This packet also includes any documents prepared while reconciling general ledger entries for a particular date.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_description: Worksheets submitted to the Treasurer of State''s office by agencies that wish to open a bank account. Such forms are submitted by state agencies that wish to collect funds that become part of the state treasury (see R.C. 113.05(A) & 113.051)) and by other agencies for which the Treasurer of State serves as the designated custodian (see R.C. 113.05(B) & 113.051). Agencies completing this form include information that includes, but is not limited to: the reason for which the account is to be created, the bank at which the account should be opened, the desired account features and attributes, and citation to the statutory authority that permits the Treasurer to open the account. This category of records also includes letters or forms submitted by agencies that wish to close existing bank accounts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain (in either paper or digital format) for 5 years from the date that the form or letter was submitted by the agency requesting or closing the account. ; Electronic: Retain (in either paper or digital format) for 5 years from the date that the form or letter was submitted by the agency requesting or closing the account.

series_description: These are schedules and supporting documentation prepared by various departments at the Treasurer of State, and collected and maintained by the Accounting department. These schedules are prepared pursuant to General Accounting Standards Board (GASB) Statements # 3, 28, 31 & 40, as interpreted by the Office of Budget and Management (OBM), and in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). Such schedules are then delivered to OBM for their use in compiling the Comprehensive Annual Financial Report (CAFR) for the State of Ohio for a given fiscal year. These statements contain financial information about deposits and investments that the Treasurer of State holds directly, or as custodian, which includes, but is not limited to: linked deposit investments, STAR Ohio funds, securities lending income, lottery funds, tobacco settlement proceeds, and other custodial funds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain (in either paper or digital format) for 7 years from the conclusion of the fiscal year for to the GASB schedules correspond ; Electronic: Retain (in either paper or digital format) for 7 years from the conclusion of the fiscal year for to the GASB schedules correspond

series_description: Electronic monthly bank statements received by the Treasurer of State for state and custodial accounts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_title: Payment Batch Reports and pay-ins forms (for CPIM revenue)
series_description: Reports that list and document revenue received by the CPIM program as payment for certification and program registration fees. Such revenue is delivered to and deposited through the Treasurer of State''s Revenue Management department, and is accompanied by a pay-in form.
retention_statement: Paper: Retained by CPIM for one calendar year following the conclusion of the fiscal year for which the corresponding revenue is deposited through the Treasurer of State''s Revenue Management department, provided that an audit report issued by the Auditor of State for the Treasurer of State for the corresponding time period has been issued.

series_description: Statements submitted by a depository bank that describe collateral pledged by that bank to the Treasurer as security for public moneys deposited in that bank by the Treasurer.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year to which statement pertains, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: On a monthly basis, Trustees that hold securities pledged by depository banks send a statement to the Treasurer that details the inventory of all securities held by that Trustee for the benefit of the Treasurer. These statements are then reconciled against a report that lists the Treasurer''s inventory of securities pledged to collateralize deposits. Results of this reconciliation are recorded on a summary report. All three of these documents are stored together.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year to which report pertains, plus an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year to which report pertains, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Monthly reports generated from Treasurer''s computer system that list all agency collateral transactions and activity within a given month. This report lists information for all pledges, releases, increases or decreases in pledge amounts, and updates to records resulting from submission of Account Verification Request Forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which the report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Pledge Release documents are comprised of several forms and reports. When an agency wishes to return pledged collateral to a private entity that is held by the Treasurer (see SEC-701), the agency submits a Request for Release form. Data from these forms are manually entered into the Treasurer''s computer system. A preliminary report called a Request Release Report is generated. The information on this preliminary report is then verified for accuracy before the Release Report is generated. Separate reports are generated for each agency to which pledged securities are released on any given business day. The Release Report is signed by the agency official to whom the pledged security is released. Supporting documentation is also stored with these reports. Such documents may include, but are not limited to, copies of the Request for Release form, copies of corresponding Pledge Form, copies of applications submitted by pledgor to agency, copies of the security, Bloomberg description of the security, and copy of the state ID of the agency employee retrieving the released security.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which the agency collateral was released, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Private entities required to pledge securities to an agency may elect to pledge a certificate of deposit. As the CD approaches maturity, this form is sent to the financial institution, which is instructed to provide confirmation of the deposit and whether or not the CD was renewed, and the terms on which the CD was renewed.
retention_statement: Paper: Forms are retained with pledged security for duration that the pledge is active and security is held in custody by Treasurer. Once corresponding security is released to agency, form is stored with Pledge Release Documents (SEC-702) until conclusion of fiscal year in which the agency collateral was released, plus an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Forms are retained with pledged security for duration that the pledge is active and security is held in custody by Treasurer. Once corresponding security is released to agency, form is stored with Pledge Release Documents (SEC-702) until conclusion of fiscal year in which the agency collateral was released, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Summary of Collateral Sufficiency Report & supporting documentation
series_description: Final two pages of Collateral Sufficiency Report (see SEC-604a) are printed each day. These pages of the report list whether or not execution of computer formulas found collateral deficiencies at any of the depository banks, and identifies any such deficiencies. Supporting documentation showing resolution of any identified deficiencies is attached.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Forms Documenting Release or Substitution of Pledged Collateral by Depository Banks
series_description: Forms submitted by depository banks to the Treasurer that request the return of securities previously pledged to collateralize public deposits held by that bank. R.C. Chapter 135 requires that depository banks pledge collateral as security in return for the deposit of public funds. Banks will ask to substitute collateral if the pledged security is maturing, or ask for the collateral to be returned if the bank has pledged collateral in excess of that required in R.C. Chapter 135. After this form is received by the Treasurer, a collateral sufficiency report is generated for that bank which indicates whether the release of the specified collateral will result in non-compliance with the collateralization requirements in R.C. Chapter 135. If the release or substitution is approved, then a release confirmation is obtained from the Trustee that shows that the pledged security was returned to the depository bank. All three of these documents are stored together.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which form was filed, plus an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which form was filed, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: This report shows all collateral that was pledged to the Treasurer or released back to depository banks on any given business day. R.C. Chapter 135 requires that depository banks collateralize deposits of public funds. A computer script automatically generates this report at the conclusion of each business day.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Confirmations of collateral pledged by depository banks to trustees
series_description: Depository banks pledge required collateral directly to qualified trustees pursuant to R.C. Chapter 135. Trustees then send a confirmation of the receipt of such pledge directly to the Treasurer. The confirmation generally includes a description of the pledged security, the amount of the security, maturity date, interest rate, and names of the pledgor and pledgee.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for duration that collateral remains pledged and active; once the collateral is released or substituted, retain confirmation with corresponding forms documenting Release or Substitution of Pledged Collateral (SEC-603) until the conclusion of the fiscal year in which the collateral is released, plus 4 additional fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain for duration that collateral remains pledged and active; once the collateral is released or substituted, retain confirmation with corresponding forms documenting Release or Substitution of Pledged Collateral (SEC-603) until the conclusion of the fiscal year in which the collateral is released, plus 4 additional fiscal years.

series_title: Pledge Reports & Pending Pledge Reports (Agency Collateral)
series_description: When a private entity engages in certain activities within the state, Ohio law requires that securities be pledged to the Ohio Treasury for the benefit of the agency that regulates such activities. These securities are held in trust and indemnify the relevant agency in the event that a pledgor defaults on an obligation arising from the performance of the specific activity. The Pending Pledge Report shows the information as entered into the Treasurer''s computer system for each security pledged to a state agency and deposited with the Treasurer. Once this information is verified for accuracy, record numbers are assigned to the pledges, and the Pledge Report is generated. Separate reports are generated for each agency submitting pledged securities to the Treasurer on any given business day. These reports list the name of the pledgor, pledgee, and details about the pledged security (type, amount, maturity date, CUSIP).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which the reports were generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Reconciliation of investment income records to pay-in documents
series_description: When investment income is deposited in a state-owned bank account, a pay-in form is completed by the Trust department which instructs the Office of Budget & Management (OBM) to credit the income to the appropriate fund. This pay-in document is reconciled against a report generated from the investment inventory software to confirm that all investment income has been properly recorded on the pay-in form. Copies of both the pay-in summary downloaded from OAKS and the report generated by the investment inventory software are retained along with any supporting documentation that would explain any reconciliation discrepancies.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Linked Deposit program daily activity and reconciliation documents
series_description: A packet of documents that includes a report from the investment inventory software that shows all linked deposit purchases, maturities and interest paid on a given day. This report is then reconciled against bank documents, which are also included in this packet, and that show the incoming and outgoing funds for the corresponding business day.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Daily statements of custodial STAR Ohio accounts
series_description: A packet of documents that includes a report from the investment inventory software that shows all contributions, withdrawals, and interest paid on a given day for STAR Ohio assets held by funds for which the Treasurer acts as custodian. This report is then reconciled against statements from the STAR Ohio web site, which are also included in this packet, and that show contributions and withdrawals from STAR Ohio for the corresponding business day.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Monthly statements of custodial STAR Ohio and investment accounts
series_description: Reports generated from the investment inventory software that show all transactions for the previous calendar month in STAR Ohio accounts and investment accounts for which the Treasurer acts as custodian. Monthly statements received in the mail or printed from bank websites show activity that occurred in each account. The bank statements are then reconciled to the reports generated from the investment inventory software. Supporting documentation and emails detailing any exceptions or issues are included with these monthly reports and statements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Trade confirmations and purchase and maturity notices
series_description: Paper confirmations provided by brokers that document securities purchased and sold by the Treasurer of State. Also includes purchase and maturity notices sent by banks to inform the Treasurer that certificates of deposits purchased as part of the linked deposit program are approaching maturity. Note that not all institutions send paper notices.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Investment daily activity and bank reconciliation documents
series_description: Each business day, a packet is compiled that consists of various reports that the Trust Department uses to reconcile the Treasurer''s records of daily investment activities against records maintained by the bank that holds the state''s main account. Reports found in this packet include, but are not necessarily limited to, the following: Settlement of Trade Report shows trades, calls, or maturities that settled on the relevant business day. Holding and Transaction Exceptions Report compares Treasurer''s records with bank records and identifies any reporting discrepancies. The Retro Report shows amounts that swept the previous evening from investment accounts to the main account. Failed trade reports are sent each day by the bank and list any failed trades or lack thereof. Printouts from bank website of previous day activities for the main state bank account are used to reconcile what appears on the Settlement of Trade Report. Printouts of relevant emails are also included in the packet.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Reports provided to Auditor of State
series_description: At the conclusion of each state fiscal year, the Auditor of State performs a financial audit of the Treasurer of State. As part of this audit, various reports are requested from the Trust Department. These reports show all purchases and sales of investments during the previous fiscal year, as well as investments that matured or were called for redemption. There are also reports that show the inventory of investments held by the Treasurer at the conclusion of each fiscal year, which occurs on June 30. The format of the reports can vary from year to year depending on specific requests received from auditor. The Trust Department retains digital copies of all reports provided to the Auditor of State.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 4 fiscal years from the conclusion of the fiscal year to which the reports pertain, provided that Auditor of State has completed audit of Treasurer of State and published final report for the respective fiscal year.

series_description: Documents that indicate when a bank wishes to call a certificate of deposit purchased by the Treasurer prior to its original maturity date. This occurs when a linked deposit program participant repays its bank loan prior to the loan''s original payoff date. The certificate of deposit that is linked to the specific loan is then called by the bank. Supporting documents and emails are also attached to the report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Reconciliation documents for the entire STAR Ohio investment fund
series_description: A packet of documents that includes a report from the investment inventory software that shows the daily holdings and daily transactions for a given day for the entire STAR Ohio investment fund, which is managed by the Treasurer pursuant to R.C. 135.45 These reports are reconciled against bank data that is downloaded from a BAI file into an MS Access database. Supporting documentation is also included, when necessary, to explain any discrepancies or exceptions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Documents that indicate if additional interest on the Treasurer''s investments is due to the Treasurer from the relevant bank. Additional interest is often due when a bank is late in wiring money to the Treasurer upon the maturity of a CD purchased through a linked deposit program. This report is comprised of a Linked Deposit Amortization Form, Compensation Due Report, emails, and other supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Daily reports generated by the investment inventory software that indicate whether or not any investment entries were deactivated on a given business day. If there are deactivated records, then supporting emails and other supporting documentation will be attached to the report. Investment entries can be deactivated due to certain entry errors, which can only be remedied by deleting the entry and creating a brand new entry.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Pay-in and supporting documentation packet for daily income posted by Trust Dept.
series_description: Each business day, the Trust Department prepares and files with the state cashier, a pay-in form that documents the receipt of revenue by the various investment accounts managed by the Treasurer of State. The pay-in document shows the specific OAKS fund into which the investment income is deposited. The Trust Department retains a copy of this pay-in and affixes a copy of the receipt issued by the state cashier. Documentation to support the total deposit listed on the pay-in is also included in this packet. Such documentation generally includes, but is not necessarily limited to, the following reports: Revenue Receipt Report, Access P&I Report (Principal & Interest Report from MS Access database), Transaction Capture Report (QTCR), Transaction Posting Map, and Pending Verification Balance Spreadsheet. These reports show what both the bank and the Treasurer expect to post as investment income on any given day, as well as the actual income posted by the Treasurer''s investment inventory software. The reports also show which investments are projected to mature or pay interest on a given day.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: A report that lists by depository bank each deposit account held by the Treasurer for which collateralization is required pursuant to R.C. Chapter 135, as well as the collateral pledged by that respective bank. Such deposits include, but are not limited to, certificates of deposits purchased by the Treasurer for linked deposit programs, as well as money market and other accounts and CDs purchased with State and Agency funds. This report separately lists the balance of each account and accrued interest. Pledged collateral is listed by CUSIP, and separately lists the par value and accrued interest. After listing the inventory of accounts and collateral for each bank, the report contains the results of computer-calculated formulas that indicate whether the inventory of deposits for the bank is sufficiently collateralized. The final two pages of the report list whether or not the computer program found collateral deficiencies at any of the depository banks, and identifies any such deficiencies. This report is automatically generated after the conclusion of each business day by a computer script.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain complete report in digital format for whichever of the following periods is longer: (a) one calendar year; or (b) until annual fiscal year audit of Treasurer is performed by Auditor of State, and corresponding audit report is published.

series_title: Invoices received for financial services pertaining to investment accounts.
series_description: Copies of invoices outlining fees billed to the Treasurer for financial services pertaining to the maintenance of investment accounts and purchase of daily pricing information on investment securities. Attached to the invoices are supporting documents that are used to reconcile invoiced fees to services actually provided to the Treasurer.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Recordings taken by security cameras placed throughout the offices of the Treasurer of State.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for thirty days from date of recording. Recordings older than thirty days are automatically overwritten with new footage.

series_title: Forgeries or alterations made to state warrants and checks
series_description: At times, financial institutions make payment upon the presentation of state warrant or checks that are subsequently determined to be forged, altered, or presented for payment by someone other than an authorized payee. Documents in this record series include, but are not limited to: correspondence between the agency that issued the warrant, the Treasurer''s office, and the bank where the instrument was presented for payment, copies of the forgery or alteration, copies of any reimbursement check from the bank of first presentment, pay-in documentation to show the reimbursement check was deposited into the correct account, and affidavits in support of applications for the issuance of replacement warrants.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years.

series_description: Statements that provide details on the monthly bank fees assessed on each bank account holding state funds or funds for which the Treasurer of State acts as custodian. These statements provide details on the types of banking services used by each account, the quantity of services purchased, and the fees assessed for each service. Records in this series may also include documents and spreadsheets created by the Treasurer''s office to analyze these statements. Please note that this record series does not include the monthly bank statements that provide details on each individual transaction. Monthly bank statements are retained pursuant to TOS retention schedule ACC-07 (series authorization number 10900539).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until the later of the following: the duration of current banking services agreement plus two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published. ; Paper: Retain until the later of the following: the duration of current banking services agreement plus two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published.

series_description: Electronic files that show outstanding corporate action information as of the close of the previous business day. Corporate actions include, but are not limited to, the issuance of dividends, stock splits, buybacks, and mergers. Files in this series are downloaded daily by Treasurer personnel from the websites of custodial banks or are received from the custodial banks by email.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Electronic files that show outstanding failed trade information as of the close of the previous business day. Failed trades are transactions that do not settle on the agreed-upon settlement date. Files in this series are downloaded daily by Treasurer personnel from the websites of custodial banks or are received from the custodial banks by email.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Pursuant to R.C. 113.05, the Treasurer of State is required to hold in its custody a copy of the evidence of title for depository-ineligible assets owned by the funds for which the Treasurer acts as statutory custodian.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain evidence of title onsite for duration for which fund owns the asset; upon disposition of asset, retain copy of evidence of title onsite for remainder of current fiscal year and for 2 additional fiscal years.

series_title: Responses to RFP/RFI for custody services
series_description: Custodial banks submit detailed responses to the RFP/RFI for custody services published by the Treasurer. Copies of these responses are retained separately by the Trust department in a manner consistent with TOS Agency Series Number LEG-03 (Series Authorization Number 10900506).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract for which the response to the RFP / RFI / RFQ was submitted. ; Electronic: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract for which the response to the RFP / RFI / RFQ was submitted.

series_description: Electronic files downloaded from bank website that show which securities have been loaned, to whom the securities were lent, and the amount of money earned from loaning the securities. Statement also shows the assets into which the cash received from the borrowers is invested.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Monthly report sent electronically from securities lending agent that provides a summary of earnings for the previous month from securities lending activities.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Reconciliations of FTP files against downloads from bank websites
series_description: These reconciliations are performed for internal departmental audit purposes, so as to validate the integrity of the FTP files that the Trust Dept. receives from the custodial banks for custodial accounts and state treasury investment accounts. The reconciliation is performed by comparing data within the FTP file against corresponding statements that Treasurer personnel affirmatively download from the websites of the respective custodial banks. Documents within this record series include, but are not limited to, spreadsheets that show whether the reconciliation process generated any outages or other exceptions, and the cost roll forward and asset reviews.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 2 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 2 fiscal years.

series_description: Microsoft Access database that is used to track details of compliance or other issues that may arise in both state and custodial accounts. This database is used solely for internal tracking purposes by the Trust department to ascertain incident trends.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Custodial banks are required to submit a copy of their SSAE 16 report on an annual basis to the Trust department. Statements on Standards for Attestation Engagements (SSAEs) are issued by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA). SSAE 16 reports discuss internal controls in place at service organizations (custodial bank) for activities that are relevant to the client''''''''s (Treasurer''''''''s) financial reporting. Prior to 2011, these reports were referred to as SAS 70. Included in this record series is a checklist and analysis document completed by the Trust department that documents the department''''''''s evaluation of the relevant SSAE 16.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Documents related to transition of custodial banks
series_description: The Treasurer issues RFPs/RFIs for custody and subcustody services on a periodic basis. This bidding process can result in the awarding of a contract to a new bank that does not currently serve as custodial bank for the State of Ohio. In this situation, the investment assets of a custodial account holder need to be transferred to this new custodial bank. This record series consists of documents pertaining to the transition of custodial assets from one bank to another, and may include documents and analyses resulting from testing performed on holdings and transaction files prior to the formal transition of assets.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data until conclusion of fiscal year in which transition occurred, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Microsoft Access database that is used to track the number and type of instances of compliance or other issues that arise in custodial accounts, instances of claims in the custodial accounts, as well as the analysis of the confirmed reconciliations, including the dates the reconciliations are received from custodial account holders. This database is used solely for internal tracking purposes to track relevant data and trends.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Status Report Files for Custodial Funds
series_description: Microsoft Excel spreadsheets that are created each week for each custodial fund for which the Treasurer serves as custodian and which are used solely for internal tracking purposes by the Trust department. These spreadsheets track the status of weekly reviews and identify details of relevant findings. The spreadsheets may include, but are not limited to: failed trades, issues of incident, claims, and the analysis of the confirmed reconciliation of assets (SEC-106). Status documents are created daily or weekly which are used solely for internal tracking purposes by the Trust Dept. The documents track the status of the daily and weekly control points that comprise the custodial compliance.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Forms completed each week for each investment account holder by a Compliance Examiner that lists the number and types of issues of incident, claims, and the analysis of the custodial confirmed reconciliation of assets (SEC-106). Supporting documentation is also attached to the worksheet where necessary. Information from this worksheet is then entered into the Custody Compliance Reporting Database (SEC-121).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: The Trust department performs periodic site visits at the banks that hold custodial accounts. Records within this file series include, but are not necessary limited to, internal reports, work papers, and supporting documentation that was provided by the custodial bank.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Invoices for custodial bank fees, investment manager fees, and consultant fees incurred by custodial funds. Invoices are stored by custodial fund and fiscal year. Invoices are accompanied by letters from the respective custodial fund requesting payment. Custodial bank fee invoices are analyzed by the Trust department and any supporting documentation of this analysis is attached to the bank fee invoice.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Monthly Statements for Custodial Investment Accounts
series_description: Monthly statements for the custodial accounts at each custodial bank that contains information on the respective account, monthly holdings, and transactions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Each custody agreement signed by the Treasurer of State on behalf of the custodial funds is accompanied by an operating procedures manual. This manual sets forth the various procedures that each party to the contractual relationship is to follow and is an exhibit that is incorporated into the relevant custody agreement. See also TOS Agency Series Number LEG-04 (Series Authorization Number 10900503) which discusses retention of contracts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract to which the operating procedures pertain. ; Electronic: Retain two years after the expiration of the contract to which the operating procedures pertain.

series_description: The Trust department analyzes the mutual fund documents and current holdings list for the mutual fund investments held in the state treasury investment accounts. The mutual fund documents are obtained and analyzed, when revised or updated, and the holdings list is obtained and analyzed monthly. Included in this record series is a checklist document completed by the Trust department while evaluating the aforementioned mutual fund documents.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain data until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Letters from custodial investment account holders instructing the Treasurer to wire funds or deliver securities. These letters are often accompanied by supporting documentation that is retained with the funding letter.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Electronic text files that show all securities held in accounts for which Treasurer acts as custodian. Holdings reflected in the file are current as of the close of the previous business day. Files in this series are generated daily by the relevant custodial bank and transmitted to the Treasurer through electronic means.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Electronic text files that show all securities transactions that settled on the previous business day within the accounts for which Treasurer acts as custodian. Files in this series are generated daily by the relevant custodial bank and transmitted to the Treasurer by FTP.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Pitch books are promotional and marketing materials that are presented by financial institutions, financial advisors, and broker-dealers to Treasurer of State personnel. These pitch books contain ideas on how the Treasurer should invest or manage state and custodial funds and state-issued debt, or otherwise promote services offered by the presenter. Pitch books may or may not be submitted together with a response to an RFP, RFI or RFQ.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain all pitch books for the remainder of the current fiscal year in which book is received by Treasurer''s office and for one additional fiscal year. If pitch book is submitted as part of a response to an RFP, RFI or RFQ

series_title: Securities Lending Total Average Portfolio (TAP) Verification
series_description: Each month, the Trust department calculates the 12 month rolling average balance for each of the Regular and Structured accounts. This record series includes the results of the calculations as well as the relevant supporting documentation. These calculations are required to ascertain compliance with investment policies.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Each quarter, the Trust department performs its own review of parties that the securities lending agent has deemed eligible to borrow securities through the securities lending program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: This is an electronic database used to track information about external investment managers hired by the custodial funds as well as external investment managers hired by Southern Ohio Agricultural & Community Development Foundation (SOAC).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Investment Compliance Reporting Database for State Accounts
series_description: Information from the Investment Compliance Reporting Worksheets (SEC-223) is entered into this electronic database. These worksheets are completed each week for each investment account and document compliance with relevant investment policies.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Status Report Files for State Accounts
series_description: Files created weekly and monthly for internal tracking purposes for the Trust Department. These documents reflect the status of the investment compliance reviews, changes to the automated daily compliance rules, and other relevant portfolio reporting.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Copies of certificates submitted annually by custodial banks to the Trust department that indicate the types and amounts of liability insurance maintained by the respective bank. Submission of such certificates to the Treasurer is required by the respective custody agreements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Daily reports that are compiled to verify that the Accounting department received corresponding paperwork for all the outgoing wire transfers made against the State Regular account and that such wire transfers were processed according to the original request. The packet contains the outgoing wire report, a transaction detail report derived from the investment inventory software, and a spreadsheet used to compare these reports with the paperwork received from the Treasury Services department.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_title: Investment Compliance Reporting Worksheets for State Accounts
series_description: Logs completed daily that document compliance exceptions to the relevant investment policies, and forms completed weekly, monthly or on an adhoc basis that document compliance to relevant sections of the investment policies that fall outside the daily exception reporting. Supporting documentation is also archived where necessary.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: The securities lending agent sends an invoice each month to the Trust department that lists the income that the state earned from securities lending. The Trust department reconciles this invoice to collateral and borrower information maintained by the Trust department so as to verify the accuracy of the respective invoice. Supporting documentation is also included when necessary.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Monthly Statements for State Investment Accounts
series_description: Monthly statements for the state investment accounts that contains information on the respective account, monthly holdings, and transactions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Internally drafted documents that outline the rules and policies governing the investment of funds for the state account, STAR Ohio, and securities lending program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Monthly statements submitted by securities lending agents, custodial banks for investment accounts, and the external investment manager (IM) for the Southern Ohio Agricultural & Community Development Foundation (SOAC), in which the relevant agent, bank, or IM certifies that it is acting in compliance with relevant investment policies.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: GASB Reports Prepared by Trust Dept.
series_description: Copies of GASB reports and supporting documentation compiled each year by the Compliance section of the Trust department and subsequently transmitted to the Accounting department. The Accounting department collects all GASB reports prepared by Treasurer of State departments and sends the entire package to the Office of Budget & Management (OBM) for their use in compiling the Comprehensive Annual Financial Report (CAFR) for the State of Ohio for a given fiscal year. Accounting retains copies of the entire GASB package sent to OBM pursuant to retention schedule ACC-05 (series authorization number 10900538).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 fiscal years from the conclusion of the fiscal year to which the reports pertain, provided that Auditor of State has completed audit of Treasurer of State and published final report for the respective fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain for 2 fiscal years from the conclusion of the fiscal year to which the reports pertain, provided that Auditor of State has completed audit of Treasurer of State and published final report for the respective fiscal year.

series_title: Applications to participate in Economic Development programs
series_description: Completed applications submitted to the Economic Development department by individuals that wish to participate in one of the linked-deposit programs administered by the department. Documentation includes applicant information, loan details, compliance certifications, and other information that varies based on the specific linked-deposit program to which the applicant is applying.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain applications until the later of (a) conclusion of fiscal year in which application was submitted, plus an additional 3 fiscal years, or (b) conclusion of fiscal year in which corresponding loan for an approved application was scheduled for final payment plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain applications until the later of (a) conclusion of fiscal year in which application was submitted, plus an additional 3 fiscal years, or (b) conclusion of fiscal year in which corresponding loan for an approved application was scheduled for final payment plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_title: Wire Requests from Economic Development department
series_description: Wire request sheets are prepared by Economic Development staff and submitted to the Investments and Trust department. These forms authorize the purchase of a financial instrument from the bank that is providing the linked-deposit loan to an Economic Development program participating bank. Documentation includes position requests, wire sheets, and collateral sufficiency analysis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until later of (a) conclusion of fiscal year in which corresponding wire transfer was requested, plus an additional 2 fiscal years, or (b) until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published.

series_description: Documents outlining the policies and procedures of each linked-deposit program administered by the Economic Development department.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded by revised or updated procedures.

series_description: Digital audio recordings of voicemail messages left on digital accounts the correspond to each desk phone and/or phone number assigned to a TOS employee.
retention_statement: Electronic: After 30 days, voicemail messages are automatically deleted by the phone system, if not previously deleted by recipient as a transient record.

series_description: Forms submitted by employees of state agencies that are requesting varying levels of online access to state-owned bank accounts. Also includes documents and forms related to the granting, continuation, and termination of online access to state-owned bank accounts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 2 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 2 fiscal years.

series_description: A monthly report compiled by the Investments department that lists completed department activities for the previous calendar month, including a list of investment holdings as of the close of the previous month for accounts managed by the Treasurer's office.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Monthly reports prepared by the Investments departments and submitted to ratings agencies. These reports provide information about the liquidity of the state''s finances, and provide support to the rating assigned by the agency to certain bonds issued by the state.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Daily reports that document investment activity and holdings within the STAR Ohio (State Treasury Asset Reserve) fund established pursuant to R.C. 135.45. Contents of this record series include, but are not necessarily limited to, the following reports: (1) Interest and Maturity Income Due, which shows which investments mature (either partially or completely) on the particular day; (2) Daily Settlement Report, which lists all investments that settle on any particular date; (3) Share / Yield Report from Co-Administrator, which shows the total number of shares in STAR Ohio, the weighted average maturity date of fund holdings, total value of shares purchased and redeemed each day, yields, and daily fund expenses; (4) Maturity Summary, which is a report of all STAR Ohio holdings sorted by maturity date; (5) Diversification of Assets, which shows the daily percentage of each type of asset class, issuer, or holding as a percentage of the total STAR Ohio fund; and (6) Weekly S&P Report, where the Investments department submits a weekly snapshot of financial data for the STAR Ohio fund to Standard & Poor''s to comply with the rating agency''s reporting requirements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Reconciliation of depository banks to FICA program banks
series_description: On a daily basis, the Trust department reconciles the FDIC identification numbers of banks on the Ohio Board of Deposit''s list of approved depository banks to hold state funds against the FDIC identification numbers of banks that hold Treasurer of State and STAR Ohio deposits as part of the Federally Insured Cash Account (FICA) program. The purpose of this reconciliation is to ensure depository banks are not holding state funds that are either uncollateralized or uninsured. Each day a report is generated that shows any exceptions or lack thereof. Any reports that show exceptions also contain supporting documentation as to the resolution of that exception.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until the later of the following: the conclusion of current fiscal year and for two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published. ; Paper: Retain until the later of the following: the conclusion of current fiscal year and for two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published.

series_title: Deposits held by bank compared to total bank assets and deposits requested (BANK MAX)
series_description: Weekly reports generated by the Trust department used to verify that banks on the Ohio Board of Deposit''s list of approved depository banks to hold state funds (1) do not hold state deposits in an amount that exceeds the maximum amount requested by the bank, and (2) do not hold state deposits in excess of 30% of the bank''s total assets, as required by R.C. 135.03.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until the later of the following: the conclusion of current fiscal year and for two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published. ; Electronic: Retain until the later of the following: the conclusion of current fiscal year and for two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published.

series_description: Electronic files that show all securities held in state investment accounts for which the Treasurer of State is the designated provider of investment services. Such accounts include, but are not necessarily limited to: the state regular account, STAR Ohio, and securities lending. Holdings reflected in the file are current as of the close of the previous business day, and a separate file is created each day for each account. Files in this series are automatically generated on a daily basis by the investment inventory management software.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Data in investment inventory management software
series_description: Data about each investment holding is entered into the investment inventory management software when the investment is purchased. Such data includes, but is not limited to: CUSIP, internal fund to which security is allocated, security type, description, interest rate, maturity date, par value, trade price, and broker used. After such data is entered into the software, a tracking number is automatically assigned to the record of that investment holding. The record of an investment holding is updated when interest payments are received, the security is sold, or other activity occurs. The data input into this software is used to generate a variety of reports that are covered by other retention schedules. Such reports include, but are not limited to: holding reports and transaction activity reports for various time periods.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data for relevant investment holding for six years after final sale, maturity, or disposition of that investment holding.

series_title: Individual employee network folders or drives
series_description: General correspondence, drafts, transient files, non-records, and copies of other files that employees store in a folder or portion of the network server allocated to each individual employee. This retention schedule DOES NOT apply to departmental or agency-level folders or network drives.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for two years at the end of the employee's employment.

series_description: When the state issues lease-appropriation bonds, the benefitting state agencies (either Department of Rehabilitation & Corrections (DRC) or Department of Youth Services (DYS)) may enter into subleases with local governments to facilitate funding assistance to local correctional facilities. One sublease may apply to multiple bond issuances.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for duration of corresponding bond issuance with the latest date. See DEBT-3. ; Electronic: Retain for duration of corresponding bond issuance with the latest destruction date. See DEBT-3.

series_description: For certain custodial accounts, holdings recorded in the investment inventory software are reconciled each business day against data provided or downloaded from the STAR Ohio custodial bank and other depository banks.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Printed copies of quarterly reports prepared by a third-party vendor that contain information and analysis on the financial safety and stability of financial institutions approved by the Ohio Board of Deposit to hold public funds. This record series may also contain summary reports and other work papers that document the review of these reports for compliance purposes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for 1 additional fiscal year. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for 1 additional fiscal year.

series_description: A packet of documents that compares the level of the collateral versus the daily balances to verify sufficiency of collateral.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: A packet of documents used to monitor the collateral sufficiency of delivery versus payment (DVP) and tri-party repurchase agreements. A report is generated in the investment inventory software for DVP repurchase agreements. A spreadsheet is created for tri-party repurchase agreements using the trade ticket information and the daily market value of collateral provided by the custodian. Such reports are stored in IT's Data Warehouse/SQL database.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Weekly review of non-Ohio municipal bond ratings
series_description: A spreadsheet kept electronically that tracks the ratings for municipal bonds pledged as collateral that were issued by political subdivisions outside of Ohio.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Weekly holdings reconciliations of custodial STAR Ohio accounts
series_description: A packet of documents that includes a report from the investment inventory software that shows all holdings for STAR Ohio assets held by funds for which the Treasurer acts as custodian. This report is then reconciled against a statement provided by the STAR Ohio administrators. STAR Ohio is an acronym for State Treasury Asset Reserve of Ohio, which is a local government investment fund offered by the Treasurer''s office pursuant to R.C. 135.45.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_title: Individual loan files for workforce development revolving loan fund
series_description: A digital packet of documents for each loan issued through the program that includes the loan application, promissory note, disclosure form, payment history, Quickbooks student loan ledger, completion certificate or dropout certificate, and notation of full payment.
