retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until 5 years after either (a) the loan has been paid off, or (b) the loan has been written off by the Ohio Attorney General’s Office.

series_title: Periodic reports for workforce development revolving loan fund
series_description: Monthly, quarterly, and annual reports prepared and provided by loan administrators that include, but are not limited to: the number of loan accounts pending ; the number of open and active loan accounts; the number of loan accounts in a grace period; the number of loan accounts in a deferment period (if applicable); the number of delinquent loan accounts; the number of loan accounts in default; and the number of closed loan accounts.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Monthly, quarterly, and annual reports prepared and provided by the Plan Manager, Investment Advisor, Investment Manager, Custodian and Prepaid Card Service Provider that include, but are not limited to: the number of open and active STABLE accounts; the number of STABLE accounts closed; the number of open and active STABLE Card accounts; the number of STABLE Card accounts closed; the contributions, withdrawals, and interest of investment accounts; evaluation of performance of the Plan''''''''s investment portfolios, and benchmarking; an analysis of the Plan''''''''s investment portfolios against similar program investment options; analysis and summary of recent events in the financial markets; analysis of the allocation of the Plan''''''''s investments across asset classes and/or managers and changes in allocation; and other similar information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Award agreement between the institution and TOS that details the terms and conditions of the program.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for duration of the Award Agreement plus two years.

series_description: Monthly reports and reconciliations to track the program''s operations. Such reports include, but are not limited to, a completion report, repayment report, payment tracker, loans disbursed report, reconciliations, and other similar reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Reports created to document a visit to verify an institution''s compliance on the visit tracking sheet: such information shall include, but is not limited to, that students are completing the program, correctly reporting fiscal years disbursements, entrance counseling, etc.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Electronic files received by TOS that are processed into a TOS system including, but not limited to, holdings and transactions files, bank prior day transactions, bank intraday file, custodial files, collateral holdings, collateral transactions, state holdings, state transactions, bank fee files, OPCS bank files, and other similar files. This schedule does not include the retention of data within the system or database.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for two years upon creation of the file.

series_description: Images of checks or other records of payments for participants in the workforce development program.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 7 years.

series_title: Documentation provided to the Attorney General
series_description: Documentation, including, but not limited to, participant student account documents (participant''s application, master promissory note, statement of participant account, or other similar statements demonstrating proof of the participant''s participation in the program) provided to the Attorney General, upon request, for participants in default.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: The contents of the Application and Agreements for the Ohio Pooled Collateral System may include: (1) application / agreement form; (2) trustee agreements (if collateral is held by a trustee); (3) print-outs of any email correspondence between TOS and the bank regarding the application; (4) bank information gathered from FDIC database; (5) copies of letters sent to banks informing them of the approval of applications; and (5) checklists used by TOS to determine that all required documentation is present.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year of the agreement''s termination, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Statements submitted by a Trustee that describe collateral pledged by that bank to the Treasurer as security for public deposits for banks in the Ohio Pooled Collateral System.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Communication between TOS and Participants through the the Ohio Pooled Collateral System software.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Data or reports demonstrating evidence of public unit's attestation in the Ohio Pooled Collateral System.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year in which report was generated, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: A report that compares the level of the collateral versus the daily balances to verify sufficiency of collateral for participants in the Ohio Pooled Collateral System.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Confirmations of collateral pledged by depository banks to trustees
series_description: The Ohio Pooled Collateral System banks pledge required collateral to the Treasurer at a qualified trustees pursuant to R.C. Chapter 135. Trustees then send a confirmation of the receipt of such pledge directly to the Treasurer. The confirmation generally includes a description of the pledged security, the amount of the security, maturity date, interest rate, and names of the pledgor and pledgee.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for duration that collateral remains pledged and active. Further, retain until the conclusion of the fiscal year in which the collateral is released, plus an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: This report shows all collateral that was pledged to the Treasurer or released back to depository banks on any given business day. APS2 generates this report on the following business day for Trustees participating in the Ohio Pooled Collateral System.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: The Ohio Pooled Collateral System generated reports based on data in OPCS including, but not limited to, collateral reports, collateral sufficiency reports, deposit reports, and other similar reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: On a daily basis, Trustees that hold securities pledged by the Ohio Pooled Collateral System banks send a statement to the Treasurer that details the inventory of all securities held by that Trustee for the benefit of the Treasurer. These statements are then reconciled against a report that lists the Treasurer''s inventory of securities pledged to collateralize public deposits. Results of this reconciliation are recorded on a summary report.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Forms Documenting Release or Substitution of Pledged Collateral by OPCS Banks
series_description: Forms submitted by the Ohio Pooled Collateral System banks to the Treasurer that request the return of securities previously pledged to collateralize public deposits held by that bank. R.C. Chapter 135 requires that depository banks pledge collateral as security in return for the deposit of public funds. Banks will ask to substitute collateral if the pledged security is maturing, or ask for the collateral to be returned if the bank has pledged collateral in excess of that required in R.C. Chapter 135. All of these documents are stored together.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Documents in either paper or digital format that the STABLE department provides and shares with the public and stakeholders. This may include, but are not limited to: (1) Brochures; (2) Presentations; (3) Graphics; (4) Program Forms; (5) STABLE Card materials; and (6) Other outreach materials.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for remainder of fiscal year that documents were provided, plus the following fiscal year.

series_description: Documents created to promote and provide information on the workforce development program.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal year.

series_description: Documents generated by the Economic Development Department for Outreach and application purposes including, but not limited to, web content, brochures, outreach materials, blank applications, and other similar materials.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal year.

series_description: Reports include completion certificates, withdrawal certificates, and other similar reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: The Plan''s legal disclosure either in paper or digital format.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain each booklet, and updates, for the duration of the Program. ; Paper: Retain each booklet, and updates, for the duration of the Program.

series_title: Auditor of State Requests and Reports
series_description: The requests sent by the Auditor of State and the reports, and related documents, prepared by the Treasurer of State in response to such requests.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 4 fiscal years from the conclusion of the fiscal year that the reports or requests were generated, provided that Auditor of State has completed audit of Treasurer of State and published final report for the respective fiscal year.

series_description: Documents prepared for the review or consideration of a STABLE advisory or related board and any reports and recommendations from such boards.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 2 fiscal years.

series_title: Confidential Personal Information (CPI) System Access Logs
series_description: Electronic logs documenting employee access to confidential personal information (CPI) pursuant to
retention_statement: Electronic: 7 years after the log has been generated.

series_description: Documents prepared for the review or consideration of a board or commission of which the Treasurer of State is not a member and any reports and recommendations from such board or commission.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year and for an additional two fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year and for an additional two fiscal years.

series_description: Documents include notice of application and blank application materials.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years. ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years.

series_title: Mail Payment Batch Reports and Pay-in Forms
series_description: Reports that list and document revenue received by the CPIM program as payment for certification and program registration fees. Such revenue is delivered to and deposited through the Treasurer of State''s Revenue Management department, and is accompanied by a pay-in form. The pay-in form is stored digitally via the OAKS System. Checks are processed through Revenue Management and digitized via the Bank Service Agreement. Check stubs (paper) and CPIM Invoice Forms (paper) are sent to CPIM to be entered into myCPIM.
retention_statement: Paper: Digitize and retain checks in accordance with the applicable Banking Service Agreement. Check stubs and CPIM Invoice forms may be destroyed at the end of their administrative value. ; Electronic: Digitize and retain checks in accordance with the applicable Banking Service Agreement. Check stubs and CPIM Invoice forms may be destroyed at the end of their administrative value.

series_title: Electronic Payment Batch Reports and Pay-ins Forms (for CPIM revenue)
series_description: Reports that list and document revenue received by the CPIM program as payment for certification, program registration fees, and ACH payments. Such revenue is delivered to and deposited through the Treasurer of State Revenue Management's processing system (such as CBOSS). Records for this schedule are held in an electronic database (such as Switchbox).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for remainder of calendar year in which the form was submitted, plus three additional years.

series_description: Documents created to assist with the implementation of the STABLE Program. Documents include, but are not limited to, program data files, internal/external audit files, and program analysis files.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for the fiscal year of generation plus an additional two fiscal years.

series_description: Files which contain participating entities' data which will be subsequently uploaded into the TOS administrative portal linked to Checkbook.Ohio.gov. Files are likely to take the form of Excel and CSV files. The record is the final format, or the, "ready to upload" file.
retention_statement: Electronic: Datafiles may be destroyed at the end of their administrative value.

series_description: Various documents associated with programmatic operations. Documents include internal procedures, data analytics, help/process documents, and planning files.
retention_statement: Electronic: The later of: (1) two years after the creation of the document or (2) the useful life of the document plus one year.

series_description: Files stored in the TOS Checkbook administrative portal. Publicly available information is relayed for presentation on Checkbook.Ohio.gov.
retention_statement: Electronic: For entities enrolled in the program, one year after the creation of the file. For entities no longer enrolled, files should be retained to the extent of their administrative value.

series_description: Daily data extracts from Eagle which document TOS Investment and Trust’s daily trading activity.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 90 days after creation.

series_title: Applications and Application Materials to participate in ResultsOHIO
series_description: Completed applications submitted to the ResultsOHIO department by applicants that wish to participate in the ResultsOHIO program. Documentation includes applicant information, tax clearance authorizations, project description, and any submitted attachments. Also includes documentation used by ResultsOHIO to determine eligibility and scoring.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain applications until the later of (a) 2 years, or (b) conclusion of a pay for success contract with the Treasurer’s office, plus 2 additional years. ; Electronic: Retain applications until the later of (a) 2 years, or (b) conclusion of a pay for success contract with the Treasurer’s office, plus 2 additional years.

series_description: Reference copies of state and federal legislation of interest to an agency. Inclusive of working documents in the formulation or support/opposition to the legislation, correspondence, and any other related support documentation. See GAR-LL-03.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value

series_description: This includes bank and trustee statements, monthly checklists, Quantum reports, and excel ledgers.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years.

series_description: Application materials, credit review, and final transcript.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain data for relevant investment holding for three years after maturity of enhanced note.

series_description: Documentation in accordance with bond closing, debt servicing, invoice payments, and any other transactions. Payments may include ACH, EFT, or checks.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years.

series_description: Documents related to the facilitation of CPIM educational courses. Documents include, course registration, program evaluations/feedback forms, evaluations, and course materials.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for remainder of calendar year in which the form was submitted, plus one additional year.

series_description: Documents associated with documenting compliance with statutory requirements. Documents include, CPIM Certification of Hours, Course/Program Completion, and RC 135.22(E) Exemption Forms. These documents are currently stored in the myCPIM System.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for remainder of calendar year in which the form was submitted, plus three additional years.

series_description: Materials used by Community Education department staff to prepare for conferences and/or distributed at conferences. This includes, curriculum material, worksheets, presenter biographies, and training materials.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for remainder of calendar year in which the form was submitted, plus five additional years.

series_description: Files and records associated with constituent communications, which includes email and postal mail.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year plus 1 additional fiscal year.

series_description: Communications, including messages and instant message functions, transmitted over the Bloomberg Terminal service. This includes any reports of communications generated from the Terminal.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of fiscal year, and for an additional 6 years.

series_description: Source code generated by the Treasurer of State personnel and/or contracted personnel, for the exclusive use of the agency in systems, websites, web applications, or mobile apps.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 6 months after code is superseded or replaced, unless the code is subject to ongoing litigation, audit, or regulatory hold.

series_title: AI Staff Meeting Transcriptions and Summaries
series_description: Staff meetings that are held online through video conferencing software and an AI powered note taking tool is utilized during the meeting. After the meeting, the AI tool provides a detailed transcript of what was discussed during the meeting and/or provides a detailed summary of the transcript.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain AI summary of meeting for six months after date of meeting, unless the meeting topic is subject to ongoing litigation, audit, or regulatory hold. ; Electronic: Retain AI transcription of meeting for one month after date of meeting, unless the meeting topic is subject to ongoing litigation, audit, or regulatory hold.

series_description: This series consist of documents related to completed Human Resources employee relations forms, such as telework agreement forms, emergency contact forms, and outside employment request forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain completed forms for one year after the date of final approval, unless the form is subject to ongoing litigation, audit, or regulatory hold. ; Electronic: Retain completed forms for one year after the date of final approval, unless the form is subject to ongoing litigation, audit, or regulatory hold.

series_description: Electronic files that show outstanding tax reclaim information as of the close of the previous business day. "Tax reclaim" refers to a process by which a non-resident investor obtains a refund in taxes withheld from investment income in foreign countries. Tax reclaims arise with international assets that are primarily held in sub-custody outside the United States, but may also arise with ADRs (American Depositary Receipts) held in the United States. Files in this series are downloaded daily by Treasurer personnel from the websites of custodial banks or are received from the custodial banks by email.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Electronic files that are sent to the Trust department by either a custodial bank or a custodial fund. Depending on the fund and portfolio type, such reconciliations are performed on either a weekly or monthly basis. In these files, the custodial banks and custodial funds are reconciling their records against each other and identifying any discrepancies.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Agri-link Deposit, Mainstream, Linked Deposit, interim, Star Ohio, Legislative & Inter Office Communications, Bridge, Concerning the programs.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 6 fiscal years.

series_description: Encoding & adding machine tapes recaps.
retention_statement: Paper: 4 years

series_description: Ohio Annual Report - Treasuer of State.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 copies for 12 years. (1) Check with State Library if they want extra copies at disposal time. (2) Check with . (3) Retain two (2) copies permanently. O.R.C. 149.11 must be complied with upon publication.

series_description: Taxes collected for legislation motor fuel, cigarette excise, beer & malt beverage, highway use, corporation franchise, public utilities excise & Pr relief, servrance, ecoligical disposal of tires.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_description: Public-facing information such as news clips, news releases.
retention_statement: Electronic: 2 yrs

series_description: Calculator tapes from state agencies that match the OAKS revenue documents for check payments. Also includes check tally reports and daily deposit reports from the check processing system.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_description: OAKS revenue documents, tax certifications, records of revenue from fees for certification, and related documents pertaining to Dealers in Intangibles.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_description: Taxes collected for cigarette excise tax and all related invoices and OAKS revenue documents. Also includes documents related to forgeries and forgery investigations.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_description: Includes policies and procedure manuals.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years after revised, superseded, or obsolete

series_description: All reports produced to the Governor and General Assembly of the total bonded debt for the State of Ohio, including Conduit and Special Obligation debt. This includes Sinking Fund Reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain digital copy on agency servers for 10 years from the end of period to which the report pertains. At the time that report is published, send electronic copy of report to State and State Library.

series_description: Bond / coupon payments - all debt service
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 fiscal years after records are audited. If GO Bond Certificates, provide the Ohio Historical Society an opportunity to review before . ; Paper: Retain for 3 fiscal years after records are audited. If GO Bond Certificates, provide the Ohio Historical Society an opportunity to review before destruction.

series_description: Executive Budget Proposal: Table 2-Federal Grants and State Match Detail; Table 4-Continuation, Required or Expanded Proposal; Table 4s-Continuation (C), required (R), Expanded (E); Table 5-Equipment Schedule; Table C-1 Capital Project Proposal.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year after the end of biennium ; Electronic: Retain 1 year after the end of biennium

series_title: GAF-84 Revenue Receipts and Holding Acco
series_description: Revenue Receipts and Holding Account Redistribution. Used to deposit and record revenue receipts from any known revenue source and to hold unknown receipts for redistribution.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image 4 years

series_description: Used for claiming reimbursement for in-state and out-of-state travel. May also include internal forms and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image 4 years

series_description: Expenditures documents. Authorize payment to a vendor other than a state agency for goods and/or services received. May also include any internal forms and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image 4 years

series_description: Authorizes payment to another state agency for goods and/or services received and services as the basis for disbursing funds. May also include any internal forms and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image 4 years

series_description: An order for goods and services by a state agency from a specified outside vendor or seller (other than another state agency). It represents an obligation to pay for goods or services and formally encumbers the funds to do so. May also include any internal forms and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain image for 4 years

series_description: Ohio Ethics Commission Statement for Administrative Officials source of income, gifts, travel, investments & real property.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years after the filing deadline for the document. ; Electronic: 2 years after the filing deadline for the document.

series_description: OPERS form that is completed by an employee and certified by the employer. This form certifies the dates that a former employee worked in the office of the Treasurer of State, so that the former employee can receive OPERS credit for that period of time.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain for 50 years after the employee''s date of separation ; Paper: Retain for 6 years after employee''s date of separation, and

series_description: Files maintained by the office of the Treasurer of State in its capacity as a member of a Financial Planning & Supervision Commission. Such commissions are created pursuant to R.C. Chapter 118 "upon the occurrence of a fiscal emergency in any municipal corporation, county, or township...." Files are organized by calendar year and name of political subdivision. Such files may include, but are not limited to, the following documents: financial plans prepared pursuant to R.C. 118.06, cash flow and fund balances, relevant ordinances of the political subdivision, approvals granted by the commission for the political subdivision to issue debt, minutes from commission meetings, meeting agendas, and memos.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain files for remainder of calendar year in which document was created plus the five immediately subsequent calendar years. ; Electronic: Retain files for remainder of calendar year in which document was created plus the five immediately subsequent calendar years.

series_title: Remittance of Reparations Rotary, Court Payments & IOLTA payments.
series_description: Courts and health districts collect certain fees required by statute and banks collect the interest earned on lawyers'' trust accounts (IOLTA). These funds are remitted to the Treasurer on a periodic basis. This record series includes the forms submitted by the relevant court or health district, as well as supporting documentation showing the deposit and allocation of such funds to the proper fund within OAKS. For IOLTA payments, there is supporting documentation to show the deposit of funds into the proper bank account, and the subsequent transfer of such funds to the main state bank account, and the posting of the remittance to the proper OAKS fund.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years.

series_title: Electronic Fund Transfer (EFT) payment documentation & applications
series_description: Documentation showing the aggregate daily electronic deposits of employer withholding tax, sales tax, corporate franchise tax, municipal income tax, commercial activity tax (CAT), and other taxes into the proper bank account, and the subsequent transfer of such funds to the main state bank account, and the posting of revenue from the payment of such taxes into the proper OAKS fund. Also includes supporting documentation of allocation of incoming ACH and credit card revenue to the proper OAKS fund. Also includes applications filed by taxpaying entities wishing to remit taxes through EFT.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years.

series_title: Remittance of Boat & Motor (BAM), Housing Trust Fund
series_description: Counties collect Boat & Motor (BAM), Housing Trust Fund, and remit aggregate amounts to the state on a periodic basis. This record series includes OAKS revenue documents and other supporting documentation showing the remittance of funds into the proper account.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years. ; Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal years.

series_description: Certain types of public utilities doing business in Ohio must remit a tax to the state based on the percentage of the gross receipts. This record series includes OAKS revenue documents and other supporting documentation showing the payment and remittance of funds into the proper account.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 1 fiscal year.

series_description: Insurance companies doing business in Ohio (both domestic and foreign, as defined in R.C. 5725.01) must remit a tax to the state based on the percentage of the gross amount of premiums collected in Ohio. This record series includes OAKS revenue documents and other supporting documentation showing the payment and remittance of funds into the proper account.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 3 fiscal years. ; Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 3 fiscal years.

series_description: Applications, resumes, and related materials submitted by candidates responding to job vacancy announcements in the office of the Treasurer of State. Also includes records created by the agency used to evaluate such applications, to the extent that such records were created. Note that applications for posted job vacancy announcements are submitted through a website operated by the Department of Administrative Services (DAS), which is the agency responsible for storing this data. This record series also includes unsolicited resumes sent to the office by job-seekers not responding to a particular job vacancy announcement.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years from the later of the following: (a) date of receipt of resume or application or (b) date position is filled or an agency decision is made not to fill the position. ; Electronic: Retain 2 years from the later of the following: (a) date of receipt of resume or application or (b) date position is filled or an agency decision is made not to fill the position.

series_description: Images of warrants generated directly by the Office of Budget Management (OBM), pension funds, and other state agencies. Images are generated by the financial institution at which the warrant is presented for payment, and electronically transmitted to the Treasurer''s office on a daily basis. Images can be retrieved so long as the following information is provided: (a) name of agency that requested issuance of the warrant; (b) warrant number; and (c) warrant amount. This retention schedule does not apply to images of checks directly printed by the Treasurer''s office on behalf of custodial accounts, as such images are retained by the bank upon which the check was drawn.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain images and associated metadata for viewing on redeemed warrant database for seven years from the date that the warrant image was transmitted to Treasurer''s office. Automatic script will run on a daily basis which will automatically delete all warrant images and associated metadata that are older than seven years.

series_description: Payin forms; certifications. Records of revenue from fees for certifications of dealers in intangibles.
retention_statement: Paper: 7 years

series_description: The Arbitrage; Treasury Notes; Moneyline; Dollars & $ense.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three (3) copies in the records center permanently NOTE: O.R.C. 149.11 must be complied with when this publication is first printed.

series_description: Images of checks that are deposited through the Revenue Management Department of the office of the Treasurer of State.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years

series_description: Paper checks that are generally made payable to the Treasurer of State and/or State of Ohio and are received and processed by the Revenue Management Department in the office of the Treasurer of State. Paper checks are also scanned at time of receipt, and images are retained pursuant to Agency Series Number REV-233 (Series Authorization Number: 10900484).
retention_statement: Paper: 30 days

series_description: Form is used to establish a new record of a vendor in OAKS, or to update previously-entered vendor information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain scanned image until Ohio Shared Services updates OAKS vendor profile, and

series_description: Daily reports generated by the investment inventory software that indicate the presence or absence of discrepancies in the manual entry of maturity date, tax basis, issue date, interest rate, and first coupon payment date values against a set of pre-established rules.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Daily reports that document when retroactive changes are made to business days previously posted in the investment inventory software. Such reports are accompanied by supporting emails, documents, and printed statements. Retroactive entries are made to accommodate sweep accounts, since the sweeping of funds occurs outside normal business hours. Also, retroactive entries are made to correct input and double entry errors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_description: Calculator tapes from state agencies that match the OAKS revenue documents submitted with check payments remitted to the Treasurer for processing. Also includes check tally reports and daily deposit reports from the check processing machine.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until the later of the following: the conclusion of current fiscal year and for two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published.

series_title: Cigarette Tax Stamps - order forms & invoices
series_description: In accordance with Revised Code chapter 5743, the Treasurer sells cigarette tax stamps to dealers which are subsequently affixed to individual packs of cigarettes sold in Ohio. Dealers complete and submit order forms. Information from the order form is then entered into a computer and a paper invoice is generated and sent to the dealer so proper payment can be remitted. A copy of the invoice is also printed and attached to the order form.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain order form and paper invoices until the later of the following: the conclusion of current fiscal year and for two additional fiscal years, or until annual audit of Treasurer for corresponding fiscal year by Auditor of State is complete and final audit report has been published. ; Electronic: Retain invoice information input into database until conclusion of current fiscal year and for five additional fiscal years.

series_description: The collection of Dealer in Intangible taxes was transferred from the Treasurer of State to the Department of Taxation effective September 10, 2012 pursuant to the passage and enactment of HB 487 by the 129th General Assembly. Dealers in Intangibles, as defined in R.C. 5725.01, periodically remit taxes based on gross receipts in Ohio. This record series includes OAKS revenue documents and other supporting documentation showing the payment and remittance of funds into the proper account.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 5 fiscal years.

series_title: Verification documents for warrants physically presented for payment
series_description: Payees of state-issued warrants and checks have the option to physically present the instrument for payment at the cashier''s window at the Treasurer''s office. When warrants and checks are presented for payment, cashiers will search a database to confirm the check or warrant is authentic and then print the confirmation. If the warrant or check exceeds a certain dollar amount, the cashiers will complete a carbon copy verification form, and keep one copy while giving the other copy to the payee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 6 months from date warrant or check is first presented for payment at cashier''s window. ; Electronic: Retain for 6 months from date warrant or check is first presented for payment at cashier''s window.

series_title: Reconciliation of BAI file to bank account list
series_description: A spreadsheet kept electronically that tracks the reconciliation of the accounts listed in the BAI file to the master bank account list to ensure that all accounts are identified and listed correctly. BAI, or the BAI file format, is a file format for performing electronic cash management balance reporting. The BAI format was developed and previously maintained by the Bank Administration Institute (BAI).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until conclusion of current fiscal year, and for an additional 4 fiscal years.

series_title: Data from calls and payments placed through the Ohio Electronic Funds Transfer System
series_description: Recordings of conversations between taxpayers and operators or the voicemail account at the Ohio Electronic Funds Transfer System telephone number. This system is also known as IVR (Interactive Voice Recognition). This record series also includes a log of recorded calls, as well as payment information entered by those tendering electronic payments to the Treasurer''s office by telephone. Recordings are retrievable if requestor provides date and approximate time of call, phone number that call was placed from, whether caller spoke with an operator or left a message, and if applicable, the name of the operator with whom the caller spoke. Tax payment data is stored in this record series, but also copied at the end of each day into files archived pursuant to other retention schedules.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for one year from date of recording
